content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

February 15, 1991


Wes Van Winkle
Of Counsel
BAGATELOS & FADEM
The International Building
601 California Street, Suite 1801
San Francisco, CA  94108

Re:  Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-91-032

Dear Mr. Van Winkle:

This letter is in response to your request for advice
regarding permissible uses of campaign funds under the provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Our advice is limited
to the provisions of the Act. You may wish to consult with the
Los Angeles city attorney regarding local ordinances which might
be applicable to your questions.

QUESTIONS

1.  May Councilmember Alatorre use campaign funds to prepare
an autobiographical manuscript?

2.  If Councilmember Alatorre uses personal funds to prepare
this manuscript, may he be reimbursed from campaign funds?

CONCLUSIONS

1.  Councilmember Alatorre may use campaign funds to prepare
an autobiographical manuscript to the extent this expenditure is
directly related to a political purpose.

2.  Councilmember Alatorre may be reimbursed for his
expenditure of personal funds if the expenditures to be reimbursed
are not campaign expenses or otherwise complies with the
statutory procedure for reimbursement.

___________________
¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-91-032
Page 2


FACTS

Your firm serves as legal counsel to Los Angeles City
Councilmember Richard Alatorre. On behalf of Mr. Alatorre, you
are writing to request our advice and to seek confirmation of your
interpretation of Government Code Section 89513(b).2

Councilmember Alatorre wishes to prepare a manuscript which
will examine the history and meaning of the political ascendancy
of Chicano and Latino politicians in the City of Los Angeles and
in the State of California. In particular, the document will
examine the public service career of Mr. Alatorre himself as a
Chicano leader. Much of the material will be autobiographical in
nature, with a view toward providing educational insight and a
positive role model for Chicano and Latino youth. The document
will stress the virtues of participation by Hispanics in the
political process.

To assist in the preparation of this manuscript, Mr. Alatorre
wishes to employ the services of a writer/researcher who will
conduct interviews, compile a collection of personal photographs,
newspaper articles and videotapes, and provide research and
secretarial services relevant to this work. Mr. Alatorre will
receive no compensation for the book.

Mr. Alatorre wishes to pay for the services of the writer/
researcher with campaign funds remaining in the bank account of
the nonelected-controlled committee Citizens for Alatorre. Mr.
Alatorre also wishes to use current officeholder funds for this
purpose. Additionally, in the event that these two sources of
funds are insufficient to fully compensate the writer/researcher,
Mr. Alatorre may wish to provide additional compensation from
personal funds and subsequently reimburse himself with future
campaign funds he expects to receive in his race for reelection to
his city council seat.


ANALYSIS

Initially we point out that the Act, as amended by Proposi-
tion 73, requires that candidates establish one campaign bank
account for each elective office they seek. (Section 85201.) All
contributions deposited into the campaign account are deemed to be
held in trust for expenses associated with the election of the
candidate to the specific office for which the candidate has
stated that he or she intends to seek or expenses associated with
holding that office. (Section 89510(b).)


2 In your request for advice your refer our attention to Section
85802(b). Please note that Section 85802(b) has been renumbered.
All our references to the Government Code use the new numbering
system effective January 1, 1991.

File No. A-91-032
Page 3

Moreover, Section 85304 prohibits transfers of funds between
candidates or their controlled committees. However, the United
States District Court, Eastern District of California, invalidated
the transfer prohibitions of Section 85304 to the extent the
prohibitions serve to further contribution limitations based
on a fiscal year scheme which the court also found invalid.
(Service Employees International Union, AFL-CIO, et al. v. Fair
Political Practices Commission (Case No. CIV-S-89-043 LJK-PAN)
("SEIU")).3 Therefore, under the provisions of the Act currently
in force, Councilmember Alatorre may transfer funds from the
committee Citizens for Alatorre to any other committee that
Councilmember Alatorre may have established for the purpose of
seeking office unless such other committee is subject to
contribution limits which are not fiscal year limits.4

The Act also controls the permissible expenditures of
campaign funds. As a general rule, an expenditure of campaign
funds is permitted when reasonably related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose. (Section 89512.) However,
expenditures for professional services are prohibited unless they
are directly related to a political, legislative, or governmental
purpose. (Section 89513(b).) The services of a writer/researcher
would be deemed professional services. Accordingly, an
expenditure of campaign funds to pay for such services is only
permissible if directly related to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose.

We turn now to the task of determining whether preparation of
the manuscript is directly related to a political, legislative, or
governmental purpose. The document is unrelated to any
legislative purpose. Accordingly, we discuss only the terms
"political" and "governmental" to determine whether preparation of
the document is directly related to either a political or a
governmental purpose. The commission has defined these
through regulations. However, we have provided advice which is
helpful for purposes of our analysis.

As you correctly point out in your request for advice, we
have stated that a conference held for the purpose of educating
and informing local public officials and their staffs as a
governmental purpose. (Braly Advice Letter; No. A-90-596 (copy
enclosed)). This is so because the benefit of the conference flows
to the governmental agency which employs these officials and their
staffs. (See also Eshoo Advice Letter; No. I-90-556 (copy
enclosed).)

___________________
3     The decision in SEIU has been appealed. Our advice is subject
to change pending the outcome of this appeal.

4     Please note that the result may be different under local
ordinances of the City of Los Angeles.

File No. A-91-032
Page 4

You have stated in your request for advice that the
manuscript will document Councilmember Alatorre's political
career. This document is intended to have historical value and
inspire leadership in the Hispanic community.5 However, no
governmental agency will benefit from this publication. The
publication does not purport to provide training in the
governmental process or perform a function which would otherwise
be performed by a government agency. Therefore, we conclude that
the project is not directly related to a governmental purpose.

The manuscript, however, appears to be directly related to a
political purpose. The autobiographical nature of the document
will serve to publicize the councilmember's achievements in public
service, thus aiding his political career. The manuscript will
also enhance the councilmember's name recognition in the
community. Thus, campaign funds may be used for the preparation
of this document.

You have also asked whether Councilmember Alatorre may
reimburse himself with campaign funds for any personal expenses
incurred in the preparation of this document. Section 89511.5
specifically authorizes reimbursement as follows:

(a) An incumbent elected officer may utilize
his or her personal funds for expenditures
authorized by subdivision (b) of Section 89510
without first depositing those funds in his or her
controlled committee's campaign bank account, if
both of the following conditions are met:

(1) The expenditures are not campaign
expenses.

(2) The treasurer of the committee is
provided with a dated receipt and a written
description of the expenditure.

(b) An incumbent elected officer may be
reimbursed for expenditures of his or her personal
funds, from either the controlled committee
campaign bank account established pursuant to
Section 85201 with respect to election to the
incumbent term of office, or from a controlled
committee campaign bank account established
pursuant to Section 85201 with respect to election
to a future term of office, if all of the following
conditions are met:

5 The Commission does not act as a finder of fact. (In re
Oglesby (1977) 1 FPPC Ops. 17, copy enclosed.) Accordingly, we
limit our advice to the information provided in your letter.

File No. A-91-032
Page 5

(1) The expenditures are not campaign
expenses.

(2) The incumbent elected officer, prior to
reimbursement, provides the treasurer of the
committee with a dated receipt and a written
description of each expenditure.

(3) Reimbursement is paid within 90 days of
the expenditure, in the case of a cash expenditure,
or within 90 days of the end of the billing period
in which it was included, in the case of an
expenditure charged to a credit card or charge
account.

(c) When the elected officer's controlled
committee is notified that expenditures totaling
one hundred dollars ($100) or more in a fiscal year
have been made by the incumbent elected officer,
the committee shall report, pursuant to subdivision
(1) of Section 84211, the expenditures on the
campaign statement for the period in which the
expenditures were made and the reimbursements on
the campaign statement for the period in which the
reimbursements were made.

(d) If reimbursement is not paid within the
time authorized by this section, the expenditure
shall be reported on the campaign statement as a
nonmonetary contribution received on the 90th day
after the expenditure is paid, in the case of a
cash expenditure, or within 90 days of the end of
the billing period in which it was included, in the
case of an expenditure charged to a credit card or
charge account.

(e) This section shall not be construed to
authorize an incumbent elected officer to make
expenditures from any campaign bank account for
expenses other than those expenses associated with
his or her election to the specific office for
which the account was established and expenses
associated with holding that office.

Section 89511.5.

Reimbursement for expenditures associated with the office
held by a candidate is permissible under the conditions stated
above so long as the expenditures are not campaign expenses under
the statute. We have previously referred to Regulation 18525 for
the purpose of determining whether an expenditure constitutes a
campaign expense. I am enclosing a copy of Regulation 18525 to
assist you in making this determination. If the expenditure is

File No. A-91-032
Page 6

not a campaign expense, Councilmember Alatorre may seek
reimbursement within 90 days of the expenditure.

We trust this letter adequately responds to your inquiry.
Should you have any further questions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to call me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[signature]

By: Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

SH:BB:dg

Enclosures
